title: haplopappus obovatus
intro: Haplopappus obovatus is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves, stems, and roots. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Haplopappus obovatus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other remedies.
